key_insight: ML-driven molecular dynamics shows that pristine MgH2 releases hydrogen mainly via subsurface H2 formation, and Ni is the best dopant for accelerating dehydrogenation.
engineering_relevance: The study provides a quantitative design rule for selecting catalysts/dopants that improve MgH2 hydrogen-release kinetics for storage applications.
method: ML potential-driven molecular dynamics on pristine and doped MgH2
Surface screening across low-index facets to identify the most active orientation
Dopant screening of 22 elements with descriptor-based machine learning analysis
evidence_signal: The abstract reports systematic simulations, identification of the (100) surface as most active, screening of 22 dopants, and a volcano relation with the Miedema electron density descriptor.
watch_item: The optimal dopant rule is derived from atomistic simulation and descriptor correlation, so transferability to real synthesis conditions, defects, and operating environments still needs experimental validation.
paper_title: Machine Learning Potential-Driven Molecular Dynamics Simulations of Dehydrogenation in Pristine and Doped MgH$_2$
